verdict_reasoning: BAX is canonically a cytosolic/mitochondrial outer membrane pro-apoptotic Bcl-2 family effector. However, during apoptosis BAX translocates to mitochondria and is also documented to be exposed on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane of apoptotic/dying cells, and tumor cells under stress display BAX ecto-pools. This qualifies as cell-state-induced surface display (apoptosis/ICD). Not a TM surface receptor at baseline, no GPI anchor, no stable TM-partner tether, no narrow germline lineage, not a lysosomal TM protein. The surface story rests on programmed cell death exposure.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Strength and specificity of BAX outer-leaflet exposure during apoptosis vs. mere mitochondrial outer membrane insertion is ambiguous; surface-targetable pool may be minimal.
